name: MCP SearXNG
what_it_does: An MCP server that connects AI agents to a SearXNG metasearch engine instance for privacy-respecting web search and URL content extraction, returning results and page content in clean markdown format.
use_cases: Give AI agents real-time web search capabilities without sending queries to Google or Bing directly
Extract and convert web page content to markdown for AI summarization or analysis
Build research agents that need paginated, time-filtered, or language-specific web results
Self-host a search backend for an AI stack with full query privacy
not_for: Teams without a running SearXNG instance — requires self-hosting the search engine
Scenarios needing guaranteed result freshness from a specific search index (metasearch quality varies by SearXNG config)
High-volume crawling — SearXNG instances have their own rate limits from upstream engines
best_when: You already run SearXNG (or want to) and need a privacy-preserving, self-hosted search tool for your AI agents.
avoid_when: You need a managed, SLA-backed search API without self-hosting overhead — use Brave Search MCP or Tavily instead.

auth.notes: Authentication is optional. If the SearXNG instance requires it, HTTP Basic Auth credentials can be provided via AUTH_USERNAME and AUTH_PASSWORD environment variables. Proxy support also available.

pricing.notes: MIT licensed. Requires a self-hosted SearXNG instance (also free and open source).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: TypeScript implementation with strict types; errors from SearXNG instance (rate limits, timeouts) should surface clearly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Both searxng_web_search and web_url_read are read-only and idempotent (with intelligent caching for URL reads).
known_agent_gotchas: Result quality entirely depends on the SearXNG instance configuration and which upstream engines are enabled
SearXNG instances may be rate-limited by upstream search engines (Google, Bing, etc.) causing intermittent failures
URL content extraction may fail on JavaScript-heavy SPAs that require browser rendering
Caching TTL for URL reads may return stale content for frequently updated pages
